summary: Tests only verify tool registration (names, schemas, callbacks) but zero tests exist for the 3 new handler implementations (push.ts, validate.ts, status.ts) which contain 84 lines of untested business logic including filesystem I/O, API calls, Zod validation, and auth resolution. The status tool declares a verbose parameter that is never consumed. The ToolResult index signature weakens type safety.

suggestions.description: validate and status schema tests only assert inputSchema is defined, proving nothing
suggestions.rationale: Every tool registered with McpServer.registerTool will have an inputSchema by construction. These tests pass trivially regardless of what the schema contains. The push schema test is marginally better (checks for payload property) but still weak.
suggestions.suggestion: Either remove these trivially-passing tests or make them assert specific schema properties like the push test does (e.g., validate schema has directory, status schema has verbose).

suggestions.description: handlePush has zero unit tests despite containing Zod validation, session merging, auth resolution, API client creation, and an API call
suggestions.rationale: The old tool handlers (board-feed, creation) each had dedicated test files verifying API calls, success responses, and error handling. The replacements have none. This is 23 lines of untested integration logic including JSON.parse that throws raw SyntaxError on malformed input.
suggestions.suggestion: Add unit tests for handlePush covering: valid payload round-trip, malformed JSON error, Zod validation failure, and auth failure. Mock the dependencies (resolveAuth, createApiClient, detectAgent, mergeSession).

suggestions.description: handleValidate and readNoTicketsDir have zero unit tests despite 45 lines of filesystem I/O logic
suggestions.rationale: readNoTicketsDir is a recursive directory reader that silently returns empty arrays on errors, only reads one level of subdirectories, and filters by .md extension. None of this behavior is tested. The old validate command tests were deleted.
suggestions.suggestion: Add unit tests for handleValidate mocking the filesystem, covering: empty directory, flat .md files, nested subdirectory .md files, non-.md files are skipped, unreadable directory returns empty array.

suggestions.description: handleStatus has zero unit tests despite containing auth resolution and error handling
suggestions.rationale: handleStatus wraps resolveAuth in try/catch and returns different shapes for authenticated vs unauthenticated states. Neither path is tested.
suggestions.suggestion: Add unit tests for handleStatus covering: authenticated case returns success with auth details, unauthenticated case returns toolError with helpful message.

suggestions.description: status tool declares verbose parameter in inputSchema but the handler ignores all arguments
suggestions.rationale: The handler signature is async () => which discards all args. The verbose parameter cannot affect behavior -- it is dead schema. This misleads MCP clients into thinking verbose mode exists.
suggestions.suggestion: Either implement verbose support in handleStatus (accepting and using the parameter) or remove the verbose parameter from the inputSchema. If it is planned for later, it should not be declared until a test exercises it.

suggestions.description: readNoTicketsDir is a bespoke recursive file reader that duplicates what glob libraries do
suggestions.rationale: This 30-line function manually walks directories, handles errors with catch-to-null patterns, and has a hardcoded depth limit of 2 levels. It silently ignores deeper nesting which could cause confusing validation results.
suggestions.suggestion: Consider using a glob pattern (e.g. fast-glob) or at minimum document the 2-level depth limit. If the depth limit is intentional, a test should verify that deeply nested files are excluded.

suggestions.description: Index signature [key: string]: unknown weakens ToolResult type safety
suggestions.rationale: Adding an index signature means any property access on ToolResult is valid without type errors (e.g., result.foo compiles fine). This was added for MCP SDK compatibility but undermines the typed interface.
suggestions.suggestion: If the MCP SDK requires extra properties, use a type intersection (ToolResult & Record<string, unknown>) at the call site rather than polluting the base interface.

suggestions.description: JSON.parse throws raw SyntaxError with unhelpful message on malformed input
suggestions.rationale: When an MCP client sends malformed JSON as the payload string, the error message will be something like Unexpected token at position 0 with no context about what was being parsed.
suggestions.suggestion: Wrap JSON.parse in a try/catch that throws a more descriptive error: Invalid JSON payload: <original error message>
